ingredients.item:
You can customize the vegetables in this recipe (rau củ muối chua) to your taste. For more diverse and colorful pickle jars, try adding shallots, daikon, kohlrabi, jalapeños, bell peppers, or any other vibrant veggies. As the water content used for each kind of vegetable is different, pickling with acid has a lower margin for error because it's a quicker process.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
3 cups (705 ml) white distilled vinegar
1½ cups (200 g) sugar

3 cups (705 ml) water
ingredients.original:
You can customize the vegetables in this recipe (rau củ muối chua) to your taste. For more diverse and colorful pickle jars, try adding shallots, daikon, kohlrabi, jalapeños, bell peppers, or any other vibrant veggies. As the water content used for each kind of vegetable is different, pickling with acid has a lower margin for error because it's a quicker process.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
3 cups (705 ml) white distilled vinegar
1½ cups (200 g) sugar

3 cups (705 ml) water

ingredients.item:
VEGETABLES
1 medium head cauliflower (about 1⅓ pounds/600 g), cut into bite-size florets
2 medium carrots, peeled and cut into flower-shaped slices (see Note)
ingredients.original:
VEGETABLES
1 medium head cauliflower (about 1⅓ pounds/600 g), cut into bite-size florets
2 medium carrots, peeled and cut into flower-shaped slices (see Note)

ingredients.item: 2 medium cucumbers, halved, seeds removed, and cut into ¼- to ½-inch (0.6 to 1 cm) thick slices
ingredients.original: 2 medium cucumbers, halved, seeds removed, and cut into ¼- to ½-inch (0.6 to 1 cm) thick slices

ingredients.item: 5 large cloves garlic, green cores removed and thinly sliced lengthwise
ingredients.original: 5 large cloves garlic, green cores removed and thinly sliced lengthwise

ingredients.item: 3 bird’s eye or cayenne chili peppers, sliced or whole
ingredients.original: 3 bird’s eye or cayenne chili peppers, sliced or whole

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large pourable jug, combine all ingredients for the brine and stir until solids are dissolved.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large pourable jug, combine all ingredients for the brine and stir until solids are dissolved.

ingredients.item: 2. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES In a large bowl, combine cauliflower, carrots, cucumber, and salt. Toss well and let sit until moisture is drawn out of vegetables (about 30 minutes).
ingredients.original: 2. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES In a large bowl, combine cauliflower, carrots, cucumber, and salt. Toss well and let sit until moisture is drawn out of vegetables (about 30 minutes).

ingredients.item: 3. Rinse with cold water two or three times to remove excess salt, and then drain thoroughly.
ingredients.original: 3. Rinse with cold water two or three times to remove excess salt, and then drain thoroughly.

ingredients.item: 4. Arrange vegetables, garlic, and chili peppers in two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (here), and then cover with brine. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine. Close the lid tightly and store in a cool, dry place. After 1 to 2 days, they will be ready to eat.
ingredients.original: 4. Arrange vegetables, garlic, and chili peppers in two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (here), and then cover with brine. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine. Close the lid tightly and store in a cool, dry place. After 1 to 2 days, they will be ready to eat.

ingredients.item: 5. Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month. (If the pickles start to turn too sour while storing them, simply drain all the liquid and store in an airtight container or a fresh glass jar in the refrigerator.)
ingredients.original: 5. Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month. (If the pickles start to turn too sour while storing them, simply drain all the liquid and store in an airtight container or a fresh glass jar in the refrigerator.)
